question.text: Staggering the timing of financial goals is recommended when:

answer.text: The individual has surplus income beyond all goals
The financial situation does not allow all goals to be provided for simultaneously
The client wants to achieve all goals within the same year
Interest rates in the economy are very high

explanation.text: **The Logic:** When the financial situation of an individual cannot support all goals at once, some goals may need to be deferred to ensure critical goals are not compromised. Staggering means pushing back less critical goals to a later date. **The Trap:** Staggering is a practical solution when resources are limited, not a sign of failure in planning. **Cross-Exam Context:** Goal prioritization and staggering are discussed in X-A (Ch 1) and applied in X-B case studies. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** When goals conflict, defer the flexible ones (e.g., house purchase can wait 2 years) to protect non-negotiable ones (e.g., child's college admission date).
